name: AdaptiveTabs
description: If there is not enough container width to fit all the tabs, the tabs that do not fit will be hidden and accessible via the select with the caption 'More'. If only one tab fits, the select is displayed instead of the tabs.

props.name: allowNotSelected
props.description: Allows you not to specify activeTab

props.name: breakpointsConfig
props.description: Breakpoints config which control the thresholds of tab size

props.name: className
props.description: Class name for the tabs container

props.name: moreControlProps
props.description: Settings to control popup with hidden tabs list

props.name: wrapTo
props.type: (item: TabItem<T> | undefined, node: React.ReactNode, index: number | undefined) => React.ReactNode
props.description: Allows to wrap TabItem into another component or render custom tab

name: ChangelogDialog
description: Component for displaying the changelog. It looks like a list of versions in a modal. It can display regular versions and versions associated with stories.

props.name: disableBodyScrollLock
props.description: If true, window scrolling is disabled when the dialog is open

props.name: disableOutsideClick
props.description: If true, do not close dialog on click outside

props.name: fullListLink
props.description: Link to documentation with full changelog

examples:
<ChangelogDialog
  open
  items={[
    {
      date: '03 Jul 2022',
      isNew: true,
      title: 'New navigation',
      image: {
        src: 'https://storage.yandexcloud.net/uikit-storybook-assets/changelog-dialog-picture-1.png',
        alt: 'New navigation',
        ratio: 240 / 516,
      },
      description: 'At the top of the panel is the service navigation.',
      storyId: 'someStoryId1',
    },
  ]}
  onClose={() => {}}
/>

name: ConfirmDialog
description: ConfirmDialog is a utility component, which renders confirmation dialogs

name: CookieConsent
description: CookieConsent is a component for managing cookie consent notifications. It supports three modes: notification, base, and manage, rendered based on the consent manager mode.

props.name: consentManager
props.description: The consent manager instance that controls cookie consent state

props.name: manageCookies
props.description: To open popup for managing cookies settings

props.name: onConsentPopupClose
props.description: Callback called when the consent popup is closed

name: DelayedTextInput
description: Component for delaying user input synchronization with external state.

props.name: onUpdate
props.description: Value update callback (required, unlike base TextInput)

props.name: delay
props.description: Delay in milliseconds before user input synchronization

name: FormRow
description: Base component to place form input and label.

props.name: children
props.description: Field component itself. FormRow.FieldDescription can be used here next to field component

props.name: direction
props.description: Direction in which the elements are placed

props.name: fieldId
props.description: Id of field to correctly associate it with label

props.name: labelHelpPopover
props.description: Slot for inserting HelpPopover next to label text

props.name: required
props.description: Display star next to required field

examples:
const nameFieldId = 'form-field-name';

<FormRow label={'Name'} fieldId={nameFieldId}>
  <TextInput id={nameFieldId} name={'name'} />
</FormRow>

name: Gallery
description: The base component for rendering galleries of any type of data. The component is responsible for the gallery navigation (keyboard arrows, body side click and header arrow click). The children of the Gallery should be an array of GalleryItem with the required properties for rendering the gallery item view.

name: InfiniteScroll
description: The component is useful for creating infinite lists like Social Network Feed, or events history. It renders its children. If you scroll it to the bottom, it will show a loader and call an onActivate callback. When all the data is loaded and there is no need to load more, pass the disabled={true} property.

props.name: onActivate
props.description: When called shows loader and waits till the promise is fulfilled to hide loader

props.name: children
props.description: Content to display before the loader

name: ItemSelector
description: A component that allows you to form a subset of a list (e.g., a subset of columns).

props.name: value
props.description: A subset of the id list of items

props.name: getItemId
props.description: A callback that returns the id of an item if the item is complex

props.name: hideSelected
props.description: When true, hides already selected items from the main list

props.name: renderItem
props.description: Render element in the main list

props.name: renderValueItem
props.description: Render element in the selected list

examples:
<ItemSelector
  selectorTitle="Columns"
  onUpdate={(value) => setState({value})}
  items={[
    {name: 'id', type: 'Uint32'},
    {name: 'series', type: 'Utf8'},
  ]}
  value={value}
  getItemId={(item) => item.name}
/>

name: Notifications
description: Components for displaying notifications. Can be used on desktop and touch devices.

props.name: actions
props.description: Notifications actions (e.g. create new, mark all as read)

props.name: areAllNotificationsLoaded
props.description: When true renders a Loader instead of the notifications

props.name: emptyContent
props.description: Same as errorContent, but for the Empty state

props.name: emptyImage
props.description: Custom image for the Empty state

props.name: errorContent
props.description: Used for the Error state (the message under the Error)

props.name: errorImage
props.description: Custom image for the Error state

props.name: isLoading
props.description: When true renders a Loader instead of the notifications

props.name: onLoadMoreNotifications
props.description: Callback called when the user scrolls to the end

props.name: swipeThreshold
props.description: A value from 0 to 1 — the more the harder it is to swipe

name: OnboardingMenu
description: A component for managing onboarding presets.

props.name: expanded
props.description: Whether the menu is expanded or collapsed

props.name: progress
props.description: Number of presets passed. Changes the progress bar

name: PromoSheet
description: A component for displaying a promo dialog informing the user about a new feature in the service's mobile application.
examples:
<PromoSheet
  title="New Feature"
  message="We have a new feature for you!"
  actionText="Learn more"
  closeText="Close"
  imageSrc="https://example.com/promo.png"
  actionHref="https://example.com/feature"
/>

name: Reactions
description: Component for user reactions (e.g. thumbs up, emoji etc) as in GitHub comments for example.

props.name: addButtonPlacement
props.description: Position of the Add reaction button

props.name: onToggle
props.description: Fires when a user clicks on a Reaction

props.name: popupClassName
props.description: A class for the reaction container

props.name: renderAddReaction
props.description: Custom render function for the add reaction button

props.name: renderReaction
props.description: Custom render function for the reaction button

props.name: renderReactionsContent
props.description: Callback function to render custom reactions content

props.name: renderTooltip
props.description: Reaction tooltip with the list of reacted users

name: SharePopover
description: Sharing component with support for social networks and copy link functionality.

props.name: autoclosable
props.description: Should close popover when cursor is outside

props.name: closeDelay
props.description: Delay before popover will be hidden when cursor is outside

examples:
<SharePopover
  url={url}
  title={title}
  text={text}
  withCopyLink={false}
  shareOptions={[ShareOptions.Telegram, ShareOptions.Twitter]}
/>

name: StoreBadge
description: Store Badge can be used to place links to the Google Play and AppStore app stores.

props.name: href
props.description: The URL to the app store listing

name: Stories
description: Component for displaying stories. It looks like a carousel in a modal with given places to display text and media.

props.name: disableOutsideClick
props.description: If true, do not close stories on click outside

props.name: initialStoryIndex
props.description: Index of the first story to be displayed

props.name: onClose
props.type: (event: MouseEvent | KeyboardEvent | React.MouseEvent, reason: ModalCloseReason | 'closeButtonClick') => void

props.name: onNextClick
props.description: Action when switching to next story

props.name: onPreviousClick
props.description: Action when switching to previous story

props.name: resetStoryIndexOnClose
props.description: If true, reset story index to initialStoryIndex on close

props.name: syncInTabsKey
props.description: Key for syncing story state across tabs

examples:
<Stories
  open
  items={[
    {
      title: 'Story title',
      description: 'Story text',
      media: {
        type: 'image',
        url: 'https://storage.yandexcloud.net/uikit-storybook-assets/story-picture-2.png',
      },
    },
  ]}
  onClose={() => {}}
/>

name: StoriesGroup
description: Component for displaying group of stories. It looks like a carousel in a modal with given places to display text and media.

props.name: groups
props.description: List of groups of stories to display

props.name: disableOutsideClick
props.description: If true, do not close stories on click outside

props.name: initialStoryIndex
props.description: Index of the first story to be displayed

props.name: maxSliderItemsCount
props.description: Maximum number of slider thumbnails on the screen

props.name: onClose
props.type: (event: MouseEvent | KeyboardEvent | React.MouseEvent, reason: ModalCloseReason | 'closeButtonClick') => void

props.name: onItemSelect
props.type: (itemIndexes: [groupIndex: number, itemIndex: number], selectedFromThumbnail: boolean) => void

examples:
<StoriesGroup
  open
  groups={[
    {
      items: [
        {
          title: 'New navigation',
          media: {type: 'image', url: 'https://storage.yandexcloud.net/story-picture-2.png'},
        },
      ],
    },
  ]}
  onClose={() => {}}
/>
